intro: Rhizobolus tomentosus, also known as Rauvolfia or Snake Wood, is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family, native to tropical Africa and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as reducing blood pressure, anxiety, and insomnia due to its sedative and vasodilatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Rhizobolus tomentosus include alkaloids, including reserpine, serpentinine, and ajmalicine, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Rhizobolus tomentosus is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and extracts, often in traditional medicine and Ayurvedic formulations.
